ground_truth.title: 2024 LED Liftgate Lights
ground_truth.author: xJewishNinjax
ground_truth.main_content:
xJewishNinjax
Mar 21, 2025

I guess I'm the first, 2024 Highlander with the liftgate led lights from powerty (same as cartrimm). I wanted to maintain the ability of the light turning on and off automatically with the liftgate like they would on a 2020 to 2023. I took off the back panel and used a meter to find out which wire on the ecu harness has 12V. If you're looking at the ecu it's the plug all the way on the right and the black wire on the left on the plug (looking at it plugged in). I used a blue quick splice and to wire it to the red wire on the included plug and play harness. Tested the lights and they came on. After that I cleaned up the wiring and put everything back together. They turn on when the light gate opens and turns off when it's closed. Also you can't turn them on while it's closed. It add about 2 extra minutes to the install time and works as intended.

golftango29
Mar 21, 2025

All those rear trim pieces look like the exact same as the 3rd gen Highlander. I'm wondering if they reused them so they didn't have to remold?

xJewishNinjax
Mar 21, 2025

Probably, companies are always trying to save money

moymurfs
Mar 21, 2025

Do they stay on as long as the lift gate is in the opened position or do they "time out" at all?

xJewishNinjax
Mar 21, 2025

I'm not sure if they time out. I haven't left my lift gate open that long. You can manually turn them on or off once the lift gate is open so if you plan on leaving it open for a while you can turn them off, you just have to make sure you turn them on again before you close the lift gate.

16WhiteColly
Mar 22, 2025

The LED lights look nice, but not nice enough to give up my warranty for them.

xJewishNinjax
Mar 22, 2025

Well first off they would have to prove that those lights caused whatever issue you're having and if they can't they legally they have to warranty it. But I would also just disconnect them before taking it in if I was really worried

16WhiteColly
Mar 24, 2025

I just know that dealers do not like butchered up wiring especially if they see Scotch Lock connectors.

xJewishNinjax
Mar 24, 2025

One it's not butchered and 2 they would have to take the back panel off and if they have no reason for that then they won't see the quick splice

Mlang2000
Mar 24, 2025

Nowadays these cars can report something out of spec down to the milliamp to a dealer but I can't see a dealer declining to do unrelated warranty work because of something like this.

moymurfs
Mar 25, 2025

This is absolutely true. A head service tech came in to go over my Highlander with me. He said it's drawing slightly less amperage than it's supposed to but didn't see any problems. I knew the reason was because I replaced the exterior lights with leds.

dtown40
Dec 10, 2025

I added some of those rear door lights to my 2020. The whole job was simply plug and play and they work perfectly fine. Let YouTube be your friend.

xJewishNinjax
Dec 10, 2025

You should do some research before you post. They changed the plug in 2024 thus why I had to do it the way I did.

dtown40
Jan 8, 2026

You should read post more carefully and then you would realize I was speaking about my 2020.

xJewishNinjax
Jan 8, 2026

I know you were, your post makes it sound like you think 2024 is the same as 2020 and that YouTube would've helped me but when I made this there were no videos for 2024s on how to do this
